{{Quote|They've got you carved up already, kid. Every piece bought and paid for. Your lungs go to a Moff Ammar if you lose.|Colonel Bren Derlin|<ref name="FoI">{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/fiction/feature20081031.html|text="Fists of Ion"|int=Fists of Ion|newhost=Suvudu}}</ref>}}
'''Ammar''' was the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] who governed [[Portmoak sector/Legends|Portmoak sector]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]].

==Biography==
[[Moff/Legends|Moff]] Ammar was the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Moff/Legends|Moff]] who ruled the [[Portmoak sector/Legends|Portmoak sector]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. He was noted collector of rare artwork, and possessed a [[Flatsculp/Legends|flatsculp]] called "[[Stars and Moons]]" by a [[Chandrilan/Legends|Chandrilan]] [[Artist/Legends|artist]]. Around [[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]], Ammar lost the flatscup to the [[Sullustan/Legends|Sullustan]] [[Gambler/Legends|gambler]] [[Cunbus Locb]]; Locb desired to own the piece, and launched an intricate confidence scheme to acquire it which resulted in the Sullustan winning it by cheating during a high-stakes round of [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]]. Ammar realized he had been cheated, and ordered a manhunt for Locb as the gambler escaped. Locb did not survive, and his [[Starship/Legends|starship]], the [[XS-800 light freighter/Legends|XS-800 light freighter]] ''[[Distant Wind]]'', was impounded. Ammar ordered the ship searched for "Stars and Moons," but it was hidden in an extremely well hidden [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggling]] compartment and remained undiscovered. Ammar eventually ordered the ship to be sold at auction, but offered a substantial reward for the recovery of the flatsculp.<ref name="SS" /> During the Galactic Civil War, Ammar was critical of Moff [[Abran Balfour/Legends|Abran Balfour's]] hands-off approach to running the neighboring [[Parmic sector/Legends|Parmic sector]].<ref name="GG9" />

Ammar also enjoyed smoking [[Cigarra/Legends|cigarras]].<ref name="FoI" /> In [[6 ABY/Legends|6 ABY]],<ref name="ERC">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]''</ref> Ammar placed a winning bid on the [[Lung/Legends|lungs]] of the [[Shockboxing/Legends|shockboxer]] [[Lobar Aybock]], dealing with [[Reussi]] [[Torel Vorne]] who ran an organ-harvesting operation on [[Reuss VIII/Legends|Reuss VIII]]. Vorne would rig shockboxing fights against the [[Barabel/Legends|Barabel]] [[Tull Raine]], where the loser would be [[Death/Legends|killed]] and their body stripped of organs due to a clause in the fight contract that gave Vorne the rights to the loser's corpse. Ammar attended the fight between Raine and Aybock in the [[Dool Arena]] in [[The Broken Tusk (Reuss VIII)|The Broken Tusk]], sitting at Vorne's personal table eating [[Mudbug/Legends|Kubindi mudbug]] and drinking [[Grada]]. Due to the intervention of a [[New Republic Intelligence Service]] team commanded by [[Colonel/Legends|Colonel]] [[Bren Derlin/Legends|Bren Derlin]], Aybock managed to defeat Tull Raine and Vorne's illegal organ donation trade was exposed. At the end of the fight, Raine was knocked out of the shockboxing wedge, landing on Vorne's table. Ammar was sent sprawling and spluttered on his cigarra.<ref name="FoI" />
